output_summary: profiled datasets: amazon_small, goodreads_small, metabooks_small.
This was an initial draft of the pipeline.
The pipeline was constructed to load three book datasets and perform LLM-based schema matching to align schemas to the amazon_small dataset.
Standard blocking was applied on title and author fields across dataset pairs without using ratings, counts, or internal IDs.
Rule-based matching was configured using string comparators on title and author with Jaccard similarity and a numeric comparator on publish_year with a max difference of 1.

Data fusion was set up using a rule-based fusion strategy that keeps the amazon schema and fuses attributes with specific fusers: longest_string for most string fields, maximum for numeric fields, and union for genres.
Singleton records were excluded from the fusion output.
The fusion strategy uses longest_string as the dominant fuser for string attributes and maximum for numeric attributes, with union applied to the genres attribute.
Matching behavior relies on rule-based matching with weighted comparators emphasizing title and author similarity and a numeric disambiguator on publish_year.
Schema matching was performed using a ChatOpenAI LLM model to rename columns in goodreads_small and metabooks_small to match amazon_small.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.
Generated a new evaluation script for the current fused output.
Numeric or tokenized helper logic was updated, fusion evaluation support, csv loading support.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 46.43%; evaluation attempt counter is 1.
This run was a refinement of the existing pipeline.
Introduced lightweight deterministic standardization of key fields such as title, author, publisher, and genres to improve matching and fusion accuracy.
Normalized title and author fields into new columns (title_norm, author_norm) used for blocking and matching.
Added normalization steps including HTML entity fixing, punctuation removal, whitespace collapsing, and special suffix removal.
Updated blocking to use normalized title and author fields instead of raw fields.
Expanded matching comparators to include page_count as a numeric disambiguator alongside publish_year.
Adjusted matching weights and lowered the matching threshold from 0.75 to 0.72.
Enhanced data fusion by parsing genres into lists and using union fusion for genres to fix previous 0% accuracy.
Kept fusion strategy largely the same but emphasized longest_string for textual fields and maximum for numeric fields.
Disabled singleton retention in fusion output.
Matching behavior shifted to rely on normalized fields (title_norm, author_norm) for stronger identity signals, improving robustness to punctuation, casing, and HTML artifacts.
Added page_count as a soft numeric disambiguator in matching to improve precision.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an evaluation refinement.
The dominant evaluation strategy used was a rule-based fusion strategy named 'rule_based_fusion_strategy' with registered evaluation functions tailored to each attribute.
Tokenized match evaluation functions were used for text and categorical fields such as title, author, language, and publisher.
Year-only match evaluation was used for the publish_year field.
Numeric tolerance match evaluation was used for numeric fields such as page_count.
Set equality match evaluation was used for list-like or multi-valued fields such as genres and any other detected list-like columns.
List-like columns were detected and normalized before evaluation to improve accuracy, especially for genres.
Evaluation functions were registered explicitly per gold column, ensuring alignment with the fusion strategy.
The evaluation view was built by mapping fused clusters to gold IDs using _fusion_sources and selecting the highest-confidence fused row per gold ID.
Coverage diagnostics were added to report direct and mapped ID coverage.
Debugging was enabled with output to a JSONL debug file.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 56.43%; previous logged accuracy was 46.43%; evaluation attempt counter is 2.
This run was a refinement of the existing pipeline based on evaluation results.
Blocking was changed to use only the normalized title for blocking instead of both title and author to improve recall.
Matching comparators were updated to add publisher_norm as a disambiguator alongside title_norm and author_norm.
Weights and threshold for matching were adjusted to [0.55, 0.25, 0.10, 0.07, 0.03] and 0.74 respectively.
Genre normalization was improved to canonical lowercase tokens to enable effective union fusion and fix 0% genre accuracy.
Singleton retention was disabled in the fusion engine.
Normalization functions for title, author, publisher, and genres were enhanced for better tokenization and canonicalization.
The fusion strategy remained rule-based with the same attribute fusers, but the input data normalization and matching behavior shifted to improve fusion quality.
Matching now includes publisher_norm as a soft disambiguator to reduce false positives on similar titles.
Blocking shifted from a conjunctive block on title_norm and author_norm to a single field block on title_norm to increase recall.
Normalization functions were enhanced to remove stopwords from titles and canonicalize common genre variants.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an evaluation refinement.
The dominant evaluation strategy used was a rule-based fusion strategy named 'rule_based_fusion_strategy' with multiple evaluation functions registered for different book attributes.

Evaluation functions were grouped by attribute types: tokenized_match for textual fields (title, author, publisher, language), year_only_match for publish_year, numeric_tolerance_match for numeric fields (page_count, rating, numratings, price), and set_equality_match for list/set fields (genres and other detected list-like columns).
The evaluation code was refined to use a more robust import pattern for list normalization helpers.
The gold dataset was loaded using a dedicated CSV loader function for better consistency.
Additional numeric attributes (rating, numratings, price) were added to the evaluation with numeric_tolerance_match.
The ID alignment logic was clarified and diagnostics for coverage were printed.
A compact one-line summary of evaluation functions was printed for clarity.
Debugging output was enabled with JSONL debug files for detailed evaluation traceability.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 56.43%; evaluation attempt counter is 3.
